testingRequirements: Tests must validate user-facing behavior, not implementation details
Focus on what users actually do - integration tests over unit tests
Don't mock excessively - test real behavior where possible
Coverage metrics are meaningless - user flow coverage is everything

userStories.title: Ralph loop: Codex invocation must use `exec` mode with auto-approval (no permission prompts, exits between tickets)
userStories.overview:
When Ralph is launched against a ticket/epic with `aiBackend: "codex"`, Codex opens in **interactive TUI mode** and:
1. **Asks for permission** every time it wants to edit/run a command (user reported this regression).
2. **Never exits after completing a ticket**, so the outer bash loop in `generateRalphScript` cannot advance to the next ticket. In Claude / Cursor Agent the CLI exits after `-p` print mode, the loop's next `for` iteration rewrites the prompt file, and the same terminal window continues with the next `passes: false` ticket — that is the "session reset within the same terminal window" behavior described in `docs/universal-quality-workflow-per-provider-diagrams.md`.
acceptanceCriteria: Ralph launched with `aiBackend: "codex"` does NOT show any Codex permission prompts during the run.
Codex does NOT run inside its sandbox during Ralph (verified by Codex being able to read/write normally anywhere it would as a human-driven session).

After a single ticket iteration, Codex exits and the Ralph bash loop advances to the next iteration in the same terminal window (verified by observing the "Ralph Iteration 2 of N" banner without user input).
`codex --help` is probed; if `exec` or the bypass flag is missing, we print a clear upgrade hint and exit cleanly instead of hanging.
Unit test in `src/api/ralph-script.test.ts` asserts generated script for `aiBackend: "codex"` contains `codex exec` and `--dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox`.
`docs/environments/codex.md` documents the flag and explicitly notes we intentionally bypass the sandbox for parity with Claude.
userStories.description:
## Problem

When Ralph is launched against a ticket/epic with `aiBackend: "codex"`, Codex opens in **interactive TUI mode** and:

1. **Asks for permission** every time it wants to edit/run a command (user reported this regression).
2. **Never exits after completing a ticket**, so the outer bash loop in `generateRalphScript` cannot advance to the next ticket. In Claude / Cursor Agent the CLI exits after `-p` print mode, the loop's next `for` iteration rewrites the prompt file, and the same terminal window continues with the next `passes: false` ticket — that is the "session reset within the same terminal window" behavior described in `docs/universal-quality-workflow-per-provider-diagrams.md`.

## Root Cause

`src/api/ralph-script.ts` (the `AI_BACKEND_CONFIGS.codex.invocation`) currently generates:

```bash
codex "$(cat "$PROMPT_FILE")"
```

This launches Codex's interactive TUI. There is:

- no `exec` subcommand (needed for non-interactive)
- no bypass of approval prompts
- no bypass of the sandbox (which would itself cause a permission nightmare on the user's real workspace)

Compare to the working Claude config on the same file:

```bash
claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --output-format text -p "$(cat "$PROMPT_FILE")"
```

Claude `-p` exits after completion → bash loop iterates → next ticket. No sandboxing — Claude runs directly in the user's workspace with skip-permissions.

## Expected Behavior

- Codex runs headless per iteration, NO sandbox (writes directly in the user's workspace, same as Claude), auto-approves all tool use, and exits when the iteration is done so the bash loop can pick up the next `passes: false` ticket from `plans/prd.json`.
- Must NOT run under Codex's sandboxed filesystem (`read-only` or `workspace-write` sandbox modes). Sandboxing + Ralph's loop of `git` / `pnpm` / MCP tool calls creates exactly the permission-prompt loop we want to eliminate.

## Proposed Fix

Update `AI_BACKEND_CONFIGS.codex.invocation` in `src/api/ralph-script.ts` to use `codex exec` with the combined bypass flag:

```bash
codex exec --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox "$(cat "$PROMPT_FILE")"
```

Why the combined bypass flag specifically:

- `--dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox` is documented as disabling BOTH mechanisms (https://developers.openai.com/codex/config-advanced). This matches Claude's `--dangerously-skip-permissions` posture: no prompts, no sandbox.
- The alternative `--ask-for-approval never --sandbox workspace-write` still runs Codex inside its sandbox layer, which will still prompt/fail on operations outside the workspace (e.g. reading `$HOME` configs, spawning subprocess commands not in the allowlist) — exactly the "permission nightmare" we're fixing.

Refs:

- https://developers.openai.com/codex/config-advanced — `--ask-for-approval {on-request|on-failure|never}`, `--sandbox {read-only|workspace-write|danger-full-access}`, `--dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox`.
- https://developers.openai.com/codex/config-basic.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Ralph launched with `aiBackend: "codex"` does NOT show any Codex permission prompts during the run.
- [ ] Codex does NOT run inside its sandbox during Ralph (verified by Codex being able to read/write normally anywhere it would as a human-driven session).
- [ ] After a single ticket iteration, Codex exits and the Ralph bash loop advances to the next iteration in the same terminal window (verified by observing the "Ralph Iteration 2 of N" banner without user input).
- [ ] `codex --help` is probed; if `exec` or the bypass flag is missing, we print a clear upgrade hint and exit cleanly instead of hanging.
- [ ] Unit test in `src/api/ralph-script.test.ts` asserts generated script for `aiBackend: "codex"` contains `codex exec` and `--dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox`.
- [ ] `docs/environments/codex.md` documents the flag and explicitly notes we intentionally bypass the sandbox for parity with Claude.

## Files Likely Touched

- `src/api/ralph-script.ts` — `AI_BACKEND_CONFIGS.codex.invocation` + preflightCheck.
- `src/api/ralph-script.test.ts` or `src/api/ralph.test.ts`.
- `docs/environments/codex.md`.

## Out of Scope

- Changing Codex MCP config in `~/.codex/config.toml` (that is for interactive usage, not the Ralph loop).
- Docker sandbox mode for Codex (still Claude-only per `ralph.ts`).
- Running Codex in sandbox mode — explicitly rejected because it recreates the permission-prompt problem.

## Branch Policy

All work for this ticket must be done on the current feature branch. Do not create a new branch.

userStories.title: Ralph loop: OpenCode invocation must use `run` subcommand with permission=allow (exits between tickets)
userStories.overview: When Ralph is launched against a ticket/epic with `aiBackend: "opencode"`, OpenCode opens in **interactive TUI mode** and never exits after completing a ticket. The outer bash loop in `generateRalphScript` therefore cannot advance to the next ticket, and the "same terminal window rolls into the next ticket" behavior (working for Claude and Cursor Agent) never happens.
acceptanceCriteria: Ralph launched with `aiBackend: "opencode"` uses `opencode run` (not the TUI entrypoint).
After a single ticket iteration, OpenCode exits and the Ralph bash loop advances to the next iteration in the same terminal window.
Tool approvals do not block the loop (either via `OPENCODE_PERMISSION` env or via configured `permission: allow`).
If `opencode run --help` does not list expected flags, the preflight check in `ralph-script.ts` prints an upgrade hint.
Unit test asserts the generated script for `aiBackend: "opencode"` contains `opencode run` and the permission env.
`docs/environments/opencode.md` documents the Ralph-loop headless invocation and the permission setting.
userStories.description:
## Problem

When Ralph is launched against a ticket/epic with `aiBackend: "opencode"`, OpenCode opens in **interactive TUI mode** and never exits after completing a ticket. The outer bash loop in `generateRalphScript` therefore cannot advance to the next ticket, and the "same terminal window rolls into the next ticket" behavior (working for Claude and Cursor Agent) never happens.

## Root Cause

`src/api/ralph-script.ts` (the `AI_BACKEND_CONFIGS.opencode.invocation`) currently generates:

```bash
opencode "$PROJECT_PATH" --prompt "$(cat "$PROMPT_FILE")"
```

This is the interactive TUI entrypoint with a seeded prompt — it does not return control to the parent shell after the prompt resolves. Per the official docs (https://opencode.ai/docs/cli/), the **non-interactive entrypoint is `opencode run [message..]`**. Permissions are controlled via the `permission` config (`"permission": "allow"`) or the `OPENCODE_PERMISSION` env var (https://opencode.ai/docs/permissions/), not by CLI flags.

Compare to the working Claude config:

```bash
claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --output-format text -p "$(cat "$PROMPT_FILE")"
```

Claude `-p` exits after completion → bash loop iterates → next ticket.

## Expected Behavior

- OpenCode runs headless per iteration, auto-approves tool use, and exits when the iteration is done so the bash loop can pick up the next `passes: false` ticket from `plans/prd.json`.
- Works inside the same terminal window — matches Claude and Cursor Agent behavior.

## Proposed Fix

Update `AI_BACKEND_CONFIGS.opencode.invocation` in `src/api/ralph-script.ts`:

```bash
# Auto-allow all tool permissions for the duration of this iteration.
# See https://opencode.ai/docs/permissions/
OPENCODE_PERMISSION='{"*":"allow"}' opencode run --format default "$(cat "$PROMPT_FILE")"
```

Keep `cd "$PROJECT_PATH"` at the top of the script (already present) so we don't rely on the positional-arg path.

Also consider writing an installed `.opencode/opencode.json` fragment (or documenting one) with `"permission": "allow"` so interactive users can opt-in globally without the env var.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Ralph launched with `aiBackend: "opencode"` uses `opencode run` (not the TUI entrypoint).
- [ ] After a single ticket iteration, OpenCode exits and the Ralph bash loop advances to the next iteration in the same terminal window.
- [ ] Tool approvals do not block the loop (either via `OPENCODE_PERMISSION` env or via configured `permission: allow`).
- [ ] If `opencode run --help` does not list expected flags, the preflight check in `ralph-script.ts` prints an upgrade hint.
- [ ] Unit test asserts the generated script for `aiBackend: "opencode"` contains `opencode run` and the permission env.
- [ ] `docs/environments/opencode.md` documents the Ralph-loop headless invocation and the permission setting.

## Files Likely Touched

- `src/api/ralph-script.ts` — `AI_BACKEND_CONFIGS.opencode.invocation` + preflight.
- Test file asserting the generated script.
- `docs/environments/opencode.md`.
- Optional: `.opencode/opencode.json.example` — surface the `permission: allow` pattern.

## Out of Scope

- Changes to the OpenCode plugin in `~/.config/opencode/plugins/` — this is only about the Ralph-loop invocation in the generated bash script.

## Branch Policy

All work for this ticket must be done on the current feature branch. Do not create a new branch.

userStories.title: Ralph loop: add per-provider "loop advances between tickets" contract tests + docs
userStories.overview:
The Codex/OpenCode regressions (see sibling tickets) slipped in because there is **no shared contract test** that asserts every `RalphAiBackend` in `src/api/ralph-script.ts`:
1. uses a **non-interactive / headless entrypoint** (so the CLI exits after one iteration),
2. runs with **auto-approval** for tool use AND **no sandbox** (so the loop isn't blocked by permission prompts or sandbox-denied operations),
3. allows the outer bash loop in `generateRalphScript` to roll forward to the next ticket without user input — the "reset within the same terminal window" behavior described in `docs/universal-quality-workflow-per-provider-diagrams.md`.
Today only `claude` and `cursor-agent` actually behave that way; `codex` and `opencode` regressed silently because we only test string contents.
acceptanceCriteria: New tests fail if a future change to `AI_BACKEND_CONFIGS` drops the headless entrypoint, drops the auto-approval, or (for codex) reintroduces `--sandbox read-only`/`--sandbox workspace-write`.
Docs updated with the table above and the "no sandbox in Ralph" note; linked from `docs/universal-quality-workflow-per-provider-diagrams.md`.
Tests catch both the Codex regression (sibling ticket) and the OpenCode regression (sibling ticket) when run against the pre-fix code.
userStories.description:
## Problem

The Codex/OpenCode regressions (see sibling tickets) slipped in because there is **no shared contract test** that asserts every `RalphAiBackend` in `src/api/ralph-script.ts`:

1. uses a **non-interactive / headless entrypoint** (so the CLI exits after one iteration),
2. runs with **auto-approval** for tool use AND **no sandbox** (so the loop isn't blocked by permission prompts or sandbox-denied operations),
3. allows the outer bash loop in `generateRalphScript` to roll forward to the next ticket without user input — the "reset within the same terminal window" behavior described in `docs/universal-quality-workflow-per-provider-diagrams.md`.

Today only `claude` and `cursor-agent` actually behave that way; `codex` and `opencode` regressed silently because we only test string contents.

## Proposed Fix

Add a table-driven test in `src/api/ralph-script.test.ts` (create if missing) that, for every entry in `RalphAiBackend`, asserts the generated script:

- contains the expected headless subcommand / flag (`-p` for claude, `-p` for cursor-agent, `exec` for codex, `run` for opencode, etc.);
- contains an auto-approval-AND-no-sandbox mechanism (the specific flag or env var documented for that CLI);
- does NOT include the plain interactive TUI entrypoint (e.g. `opencode "$PROJECT_PATH"` without `run`, or `codex "$(cat ...)"` without `exec`);
- for codex specifically: does NOT include `--sandbox read-only` or `--sandbox workspace-write` (we want Claude-like parity, no sandbox).

Add a single source-of-truth table in `docs/universal-quality-workflow-per-provider.md` (or companion diagrams doc) listing, per provider:

| Provider | Headless invocation | Auto-approval + no sandbox | Loop re-entry behavior |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Claude | `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions -p …` | `--dangerously-skip-permissions` (no sandbox by design) | exits after `-p`, bash loop advances |
| Cursor Agent | `cursor-agent --force --approve-mcps --trust -p …` | `--approve-mcps --trust` (no sandbox by design) | exits after `-p`, bash loop advances |
| Codex | `codex exec --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox …` | `--dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox` (explicitly disables BOTH approvals AND sandbox — matches Claude posture) | exits after `exec`, bash loop advances |
| OpenCode | `OPENCODE_PERMISSION='{"*":"allow"}' opencode run …` | env var or config `permission: allow` (no sandbox concept) | exits after `run`, bash loop advances |

Note explicitly in the docs: **we do NOT run Codex under its sandbox** (`--sandbox read-only` or `--sandbox workspace-write`) during Ralph, because sandbox + Ralph's file/git/MCP churn recreates the permission-prompt nightmare we are eliminating. Users who want sandboxing should run Codex manually, not through Ralph.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] New tests fail if a future change to `AI_BACKEND_CONFIGS` drops the headless entrypoint, drops the auto-approval, or (for codex) reintroduces `--sandbox read-only`/`--sandbox workspace-write`.
- [ ] Docs updated with the table above and the "no sandbox in Ralph" note; linked from `docs/universal-quality-workflow-per-provider-diagrams.md`.
- [ ] Tests catch both the Codex regression (sibling ticket) and the OpenCode regression (sibling ticket) when run against the pre-fix code.

## Depends On

- Sibling ticket: Codex `exec` + `--dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox` fix.
- Sibling ticket: OpenCode `run` + permissions fix.

## Files Likely Touched

- `src/api/ralph-script.test.ts` (new or extended).
- `docs/universal-quality-workflow-per-provider.md`.
- `docs/universal-quality-workflow-per-provider-diagrams.md`.

## Out of Scope

- E2E test that actually drives a Codex/OpenCode binary — unit test on generated bash is enough to prevent regression.

## Branch Policy

All work for this ticket must be done on the current feature branch. Do not create a new branch.

techStack: Database: SQLite with better-sqlite3, Drizzle ORM
State: TanStack Query for server state

dos: Use Drizzle ORM: `db.select().from(tickets)`
Use typed schema imports: `import { tickets } from "../lib/schema"`
Use `eq()`, `and()`, `sql` from drizzle-orm for conditions
Use transactions for multi-table operations: `db.transaction(() => {...})`
Use `.get()` for single row, `.all()` for multiple
donts: Raw SQL strings: `db.run("SELECT * FROM tickets")`
String-based table names
String concatenation for WHERE clauses
Multiple independent queries that should be atomic
Assume query returns what you expect without checking

dos: Use `useQuery` with `queryKeys` for data fetching
Use `useMutation` with `onSuccess` for state changes
Invalidate queries after mutations: `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: queryKeys.tickets })`
Use centralized `queryKeys` object from `src/lib/hooks.ts`
Create custom hooks in `src/lib/hooks.ts` for reusable query logic
donts: `useState` + `useEffect` for fetched data
Direct API calls without proper cache invalidation
Manual cache manipulation or refetch after every change
Hardcoded query key strings throughout components
Duplicate query setup in multiple components

dos: Use Tailwind classes for static styles: `className="p-4 bg-slate-800"`
Use CSS variables for theming: `var(--bg-primary)`, `var(--shadow-modal)`
Reserve inline styles for dynamic/computed values: `style={{ width: `${percent}%` }}`
Define shared style constants at module level for referential stability
Use `React.CSSProperties` type for inline style objects when needed
donts: Inline style objects for single-use styles
Hardcoded colors that don't adapt to themes
Mix Tailwind and inline styles inconsistently
Create new objects inside components that are reused
Untyped style objects that miss IDE autocomplete

dos: Use `createServerFn({ method: "GET" })` for reads, `"POST"` for writes
Use `.inputValidator()` for type-safe input handling
Return typed data directly: `return db.select().from(tickets).all()`
Use `ensureExists()` for required lookups: `ensureExists(project, "Project")`
Import from `@tanstack/react-start` (not `@tanstack/react-start/server`)
donts: Mix GET/POST inconsistently
Access raw input without validation
Wrap in unnecessary response objects
Return null and let caller handle missing data
Wrong import path

dos: Use Zod schemas for input validation: `{ ticketId: z.string() }`
Return structured `{ content: [{ type: "text", text: ... }] }` format
Set `isError: true` for error responses
Use `log.info()` / `log.error()` from `../lib/logging.js`
Include helpful error messages: `"Project not found. Use list_projects to see available."`
donts: Trust input without validation
Return plain strings
Return error text without the error flag
`console.log` in MCP server code
Generic "Not found" errors

dos: Test user behavior: what users see, click, and experience
Integration tests for workflows: test components together
Real database fixtures with actual schema
Tests that fail when user-facing behavior breaks
Ask: "Does this test catch bugs users would encounter?"
donts: Test implementation details, internal state, or private methods
Unit tests for every function
Excessive mocking of internals
Tests that break on refactoring internals
Chase 100% code coverage as a goal

verificationSteps: Run `pnpm type-check` - must pass with no errors
Run `pnpm lint` - must pass with no errors
Run `pnpm test` - all tests must pass
Added tests for new functionality (ONLY tests that verify real user behavior - see Testing Philosophy above)
Used typed error classes (not generic `Error`)
Used Drizzle ORM (not raw SQL) - see DO/DON'T table above
Followed existing patterns from DO/DON'T tables
No hardcoded values that should be configurable
Existing tests still pass
No regressions in related functionality
Updated tests if behavior changed
Did not break backward compatibility (unless explicitly requested)
Manually verified in browser at `localhost:4242`
Checked responsive layout
Verified TanStack Query invalidates and updates correctly
Accessibility: keyboard navigation works, proper ARIA labels
Migration file created via `pnpm db:generate`
Migration tested with `pnpm db:migrate`
Backup tested if schema changed (use `pnpm brain-dump backup` then test restore)
Updated `src/lib/schema.ts` with proper types and constraints
Tested tool via Claude Code integration
Verified error responses are informative (see DO/DON'T table)
Updated tool documentation if interface changed
Added Zod schema for input validation
All acceptance criteria from ticket met
Work summary added via `comment` tool, `action: "add"` (for Ralph sessions)
Session completed with appropriate outcome (for Ralph sessions)
Committed with proper message format: `feat(<ticket-id>): <description>`

epicTitle: Ralph Loop Parity: Codex & OpenCode Headless Invocation
epicDescription:
Bring the Codex and OpenCode Ralph-loop invocations up to parity with Claude and Cursor Agent, so that the outer bash loop in `generateRalphScript` (`src/api/ralph-script.ts`) can roll the same terminal window forward from one ticket to the next without user input or permission prompts.

## Context

Claude and Cursor Agent use headless/print mode entrypoints (`-p`), which exit after one iteration and let the `for` loop in the generated Ralph script pick up the next `passes: false` ticket from `plans/prd.json` — the "session reset within the same terminal window" behavior in `docs/universal-quality-workflow-per-provider-diagrams.md`.

Codex and OpenCode currently launch their interactive TUI entrypoints:

- `codex "$(cat "$PROMPT_FILE")"` — no `exec`, no approval bypass. Prompts for permissions, never exits.
- `opencode "$PROJECT_PATH" --prompt "..."` — no `run` subcommand, no permission config. Stays in TUI, never exits.

## Scope

1. Codex: switch to `codex exec --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox` (NO sandbox — sandbox + Ralph recreates the permission-prompt problem).
2. OpenCode: switch to `opencode run` with `OPENCODE_PERMISSION='{"*":"allow"}'`.
3. Contract tests + docs covering every `RalphAiBackend` so this regression can't recur silently.
